title: Vocational Rehabilitation - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Vocational Rehabilitation 55 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Blindness Chronic/Severe Mental Illness ARC UMATILLA COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides connections to resources such as case management services, assistance with advocacy, disability awareness training, education, recreational programs, and vocational training. 215 W Orchard Avenue Hermiston, OR 978381738 Eligibility: Individuals who have intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families Hours: Monday 9am-noon Email (541) 567-7615 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHSTAR CLUBHOUSE CLUBHOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides people with mental health challenges a working community to focus on social and vocational rehabilitation through two separate programs. Members participate in a structured working day in which members help staff operate the agency. This program also provides peer support, and social and recreational activities. 5600 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following qualifications: -Age 18 and older -Have a mental health diagnosis -Have a referral from a mental health professional -Attend orientation -Be willing to interact with other members and staff in a respectful manner Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, including major holidays for members Orientation: 1st and 3rd Wednesday of the month 3pm Email (971) 271-7273 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPPORTUNITY FOUNDATION OF CENTRAL OREGON OPPORTUNITY FOUNDATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides life improvement services for adults with developmental disabilities. Also offers a selection of adaptive items for people with visual impairments. 541 Willamette Avenue #408 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service; adults certified legally blind or diagnosed with condition leading to blindness, or age 55 years and above and unable to drive or read newspaper with glasses.
